source: [PS-PLM] Learning Piece-wise Linear Models from Large Scale Data for Ad Click Prediction (Alibaba 2017).pdf

1
Introduction
Click-through rate (CTR) prediction is a core problem in the multi-billion dollar online advertising industry.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
In this paper, we introduce an industrial strength solution with model named Large Scale
Piece-wise Linear Model (LS-PLM).
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
To improve the accuracy of CTR prediction, more and more data are involved, making CTR prediction a
large scale learning problem, with massive samples and high dimension features.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
In particular, user-click generation involves many complex factors, like ad quality, context information,
user interests, and complex interactions of these factors.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
3
Implementation
In this section, we ﬁrst provide our parallel implementation of LS-PLM model for large-scale data, then
introduce an important trick which helps to accelerate the training procedue greatly.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Due to the common feature pattern of our production data, employing the common feature trick improves
the performance of training procedure greatly, which will be shown in the following section 4.3.

Conclusions
In this paper, a piece-wise linear model, LS-PLM for CTR prediction problem is proposed.
